Title: SlBES1-mediated brassinosteroid signaling suppresses flavonoid biosynthesis in tomato fruit

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; contributes to peel color and nutritional quality
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 CYP90B family; DWF4 ortholog
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression decreases flavonoid accumulation, whereas RNAi increases flavonoid accumulation in tomato fruit peel
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; RNAi silencing; HPLC metabolite profiling; qPCR of pathway genes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlCYP90B3 is a fruit-specific rate-limiting brassinosteroid biosynthetic gene. 2. SlCYP90B3-OE lines showed substantially reduced NarCh/flavonoid content, while SlCYP90B3-RNAi lines showed elevated flavonoid content. 3. SlCYP90B3-OE repressed SlMYB12, SlCHS1, SlCHS2, and SlF3'H expression, linking endogenous BR biosynthesis to suppression of flavonoid production.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing SlCYP90B3 activity could be used to elevate tomato peel flavonoids by lowering endogenous BR levels, but this should be balanced against broader BR-dependent developmental effects
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broader BR-related growth, ripening, and development effects due to altered hormone biosynthesis; specific yield penalty not reported in this paper
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison with exogenous 24-epibrassinolide treatment; fruits analyzed during ripening
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although SlMYB12 was strongly repressed in SlCYP90B3-OE, SlMYB12 was not significantly increased in SlCYP90B3-RNAi, suggesting threshold-dependent regulation of the MYB node

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early/core flavonoid pathway leading to naringenin chalcone formation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone/stilbene synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses 4-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form naringenin chalcone, the first committed flavonoid intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early structural step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the major flavonoid precursor and major accumulated peel flavonoid component
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit peel; suppressed by EBL and SlBES1 overexpression; increased in bes1, bzr1 bes1, SlBIN2-OE, and SlCYP90B3-RNAi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced SlCHS1 expression is associated with reduced flavonoid/NarCh accumulation; derepression in bes1 backgrounds accompanies elevated flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; genetic analysis in SlBES1-OE and bes1 lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlCHS1 is a direct repression target of SlBES1 in vivo by ChIP-qPCR. 2. SlBES1 suppresses SlCHS1 promoter activity in dual-luciferase assays. 3. SlCHS1 expression tracks with flavonoid content across BR-related genotypes, supporting its causal role in NarCh accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native SlCHS1 promoter in assays; 35S promoter used for SlBES1-myc and effector constructs in transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 24-epibrassinolide treatment; BR genetic perturbation; fruit ripening stage
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing SlCHS1 expression may increase peel flavonoids; could be combined with removal of SlBES1 repression or SlMYB12 activation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Y1H validation for SlCHS1 was impeded by promoter self-activation, but ChIP-qPCR and dual-luciferase supported direct regulation

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early/core flavonoid pathway leading to naringenin chalcone formation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone/stilbene synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses 4-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form naringenin chalcone in the flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early structural step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the major flavonoid precursor
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit peel; downregulated by EBL and SlBES1 overexpression; upregulated in bes1, bzr1 bes1, SlBIN2-OE, and SlCYP90B3-RNAi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lower SlCHS2 expression accompanies reduced flavonoid accumulation, whereas derepression accompanies increased flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; qPCR; genetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlBES1 directly binds the SlCHS2 promoter in Y1H and ChIP-qPCR assays. 2. SlBES1 represses SlCHS2 promoter activity in transient assays. 3. SlBZR1 enhances SlBES1-mediated repression of SlCHS2 when co-expressed.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native SlCHS2 promoter in assays; 35S promoter used for effector expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 24-epibrassinolide treatment; BR pathway genetic perturbation; ripening stage
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A useful engineering node to increase peel flavonoids through promoter editing or release from SlBES1 repression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of the SlBES1-SlMYB12-SlCHS hierarchical repression cascade

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavonoid intermediates at the 3' position, contributing to downstream flavonoid diversification
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Contributes to downstream flavonoid branch composition and accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit peel; downregulated by EBL and SlBES1 overexpression; upregulated in bes1, bzr1 bes1, SlBIN2-OE, and SlCYP90B3-RNAi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Repression of SlF3'H is associated with lower total flavonoid output under active BR signaling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlBES1 directly binds the SlF3'H promoter in vitro and in vivo. 2. SlBES1 represses SlF3'H promoter activity. 3. SlF3'H transcript abundance is inversely associated with BR signaling strength and positively associated with flavonoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native SlF3'H promoter in assays; 35S promoter used for SlBES1 effectors
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 24-epibrassinolide treatment; BR genetic perturbation; ripening stage
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be co-engineered with CHS genes and SlMYB12/SlBES1 for broader enhancement of flavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included among the core direct targets through which SlBES1 suppresses flavonoid biosynthesis

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Basic helix-loop-helix transcription factor; BES1/BZR family BR signaling regulator
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus when dephosphorylated under BR signaling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit peel/fruit; manipulated in OE and knockout lines
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; peel color formation; nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid biosynthesis via direct repression of structural genes and repression of the upstream activator SlMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression by direct promoter binding to E-box motifs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses SlMYB12, SlCHS1, SlCHS2, and SlF3'H transcription
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression decreases peel flavonoids, whereas knockout markedly increases flavonoid accumulation; the double mutant bzr1 bes1 shows the strongest increase
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary negative regulator and dominant BR-signaling mediator suppressing tomato flavonoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Brassinosteroid signaling; endogenous BR level; exogenous 24-epibrassinolide; negative regulation relieved by SlBIN2 inactivation state
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Hierarchical repression cascade through SlBES1-SlMYB12-SlCHS; no explicit feedback loop established
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functional cooperation with SlBZR1 in repressing flavonoid-related promoters
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; overexpression; HPLC metabolite profiling; qPCR; Y1H; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; VIGS epistasis with SlMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlBES1 is the dominant BR-signaling transcription factor repressing flavonoid accumulation in tomato fruit peel. 2. It directly binds and represses SlCHS1, SlCHS2, SlF3'H, and SlMYB12 promoters. 3. Silencing SlMYB12 in the bes1 background abolishes the high-flavonoid phenotype, showing that SlMYB12 mediates part of SlBES1 action. 4. Natural promoter variation in SlBES1 correlates with flavonoid metabolite levels during domestication.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for SlBES1-myc and transient effector constructs; native target promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA635540; DAP-seq; metabolome GWAS based on resequencing of 356 accessions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Previously linked to fruit firmness/softening; editing may affect storage traits, though authors suggest bes1 can enhance flavonoids without altering carotenoids
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for tomato flavonoid biofortification via knockout or promoter/haplotype editing; can be combined with SlMYB12 activation or E8-SlMYB12 stacking to improve peel/flesh pigmentation and nutrition
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper distinguishes SlBES1 from SlBZR1: SlBES1 mainly represses flavonoids, whereas SlBZR1 mainly promotes carotenoids

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Fruit peel epidermis during ripening; associated with yellow peel pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Suppressed by EBL and SlBES1 overexpression; elevated in bes1 and SlBIN2-OE backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; yellow peel pigmentation; nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Activation of flavonoid biosynthetic gene expression and NarCh accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of structural flavonoid genes; itself transcriptionally repressed by SlBES1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator required for high peel flavonoid/NarCh accumulation; silencing abolishes the high-flavonoid phenotype of bes1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master positive regulator of tomato fruit flavonoid biosynthesis acting downstream of SlBES1 repression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Brassinosteroid signaling via SlBES1 repression; exogenous EBL
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts within a hierarchical cascade downstream of SlBES1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; dual-luciferase assay for CHS promoters; Y1H and ChIP-qPCR showing SlBES1 binds its promoter; VIGS silencing in WT and bes1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYB12 directly activates SlCHS1 and SlCHS2 to promote flavonoid biosynthesis. 2. SlBES1 binds and represses the SlMYB12 promoter. 3. Virus-induced silencing of SlMYB12 in bes1 abolishes bes1-enhanced flavonoid accumulation, demonstrating that SlMYB12 mediates SlBES1 hierarchical regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native SlMYB12 promoter in reporter/Y1H assays; pTRV2 used for VIGS
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 24-epibrassinolide treatment; ripening stage; pink vs red tomato backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of SlMYB12 produces colorless peel/pink fruit due to NarCh deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful positive regulator for flavonoid enhancement; can be stacked with bes1 or expressed in flesh-specific contexts such as E8-SlMYB12 to improve nutritional quality and color diversity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Pink-fruited tomatoes behave similarly to SlMYB12-deficient backgrounds, explaining weak BR responsiveness for NarCh accumulation

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BES1/BZR family BR signaling transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus when dephosphorylated under BR signaling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit; tested in OE, knockout, and double mutant with bes1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flavonoids in peel; carotenoids in flesh
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Condition-dependent regulation of flavonoid repression in cooperation with SlBES1; primary activation of carotenoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Co-repressive effect with SlBES1 on flavonoid genes; transcriptional activation of carotenoid genes reported in this paper's cited previous work
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: For flavonoid context: SlMYB12; SlCHS1; SlCHS2 (cooperatively in transient assays). For carotenoid context discussed: SlPSY1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Alone has weak effect on SlMYB12; enhances SlBES1-mediated repression of SlMYB12, SlCHS1, and SlCHS2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minor independent effect on flavonoid accumulation, but cooperates with SlBES1 such that the bzr1 bes1 double mutant accumulates more flavonoids than bes1 alone
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive co-regulator for flavonoid repression, but primary BR regulator for carotenoid pathway activation rather than the main flavonoid controller
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Cooperative action with SlBES1 in transient repression assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and knockout phenotyping; dual-luciferase co-expression assays; RNA-seq and DAP-seq integration
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlBZR1 alone shows limited influence on flavonoid biosynthesis compared with SlBES1. 2. Co-expression of SlBZR1 with SlBES1 enhances repression of SlMYB12, SlCHS1, and SlCHS2. 3. The double mutant bzr1 bes1 has stronger flavonoid accumulation than bes1 alone, indicating cooperative action. 4. SlBZR1 primarily regulates carotenoid pathway activity, distinguishing its main role from SlBES1.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may alter carotenoid accumulation and ripening-related pathways
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: More useful for carotenoid engineering than direct flavonoid biofortification; may be considered in combined pigment engineering with SlBES1
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Population variation in SlBZR1 was not significantly associated with flavonoid metabolite levels in GWAS/haplotype analyses

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assessed in OE and knockout fruit peel
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: BR signaling control over flavonoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Negative regulator of BR signaling that positively influences flavonoid accumulation in this system
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Indirectly affects SlMYB12; SlCHS1; SlCHS2; SlF3'H expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: SlBIN2-OE elevates SlMYB12, SlCHS1, SlCHS2, and SlF3'H transcript levels; bin2 reduces them
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases flavonoid accumulation, whereas bin2 knockout decreases flavonoids
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive inducer of flavonoid biosynthesis through antagonism of BR signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and knockout phenotyping; qPCR of flavonoid-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlBIN2 functions as a positive inducer of flavonoid biosynthesis in tomato. 2. SlBIN2-OE fruit peel accumulates more flavonoids and shows higher expression of SlMYB12, SlCHS1, SlCHS2, and SlF3'H. 3. bin2 mutants show the opposite phenotype, consistent with BIN2 acting as a negative regulator of BR signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad BR-related developmental effects; specific penalties not reported here
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to increase flavonoids, but pathway-wide BR effects may make it less precise than SlBES1 editing
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core BR signaling regulator experimentally tested in the Results for causal effects on flavonoid accumulation
